title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: June 03, 2026: Global Tensions and AI Enthusiasm Drive Markets

items.headline: US Futures Mixed as Geopolitical Tensions Persist, AI Enthusiasm Continues
items.summary: US stock futures are exhibiting mixed to slightly down movements on Wednesday, June 3, 2026, following a strong close for major indices on Tuesday, largely propelled by optimism around artificial intelligence. Global market sentiment remains cautious, influenced by persistently rising crude oil prices and escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Today's economic calendar includes the release of the Federal Reserve's Beige Book, with investors also anticipating May ISM Services and April Factory Orders data.

sections.title: Top Movers (As of June 2-3, 2026 Trading Sessions)

items.headline: VIDA Global Inc. Leads Gainers
items.summary: VIDA Global Inc. ($VIDA) is reported as a top performer in June, reflecting strong momentum.

items.headline: Marvell Technology Surges on AI Talk
items.summary: Marvell Technology Inc. ($MRVL) saw a significant jump after Nvidia's CEO hinted it could be the next trillion-dollar company, highlighting continued strong sentiment in the AI sector.

items.headline: Hitek Global Inc. Sees Sharp Decline
items.summary: Hitek Global Inc. ($HKIT) experienced a substantial drop in the latest trading session.

items.headline: Fulcrum Therapeutics Falls
items.summary: Fulcrum Therapeutics, Inc. ($FULC) was among the significant decliners in recent trading.

items.headline: AI and Energy Sectors Show Strength, Others Lag
items.summary: Artificial intelligence continues to be a primary driver for semiconductor and technology stocks, demonstrating robust performance. The Information Technology sector now accounts for nearly 40% of the S&P 500, a record high. Energy shares are also strong, with crude oil prices rising for the third consecutive session. Conversely, real estate and utilities have shown lagging performance in the US, while the Indian IT sector experienced a significant decline of 5.57%. There is growing interest among investors for cyclicals, small caps, financials, and industrials, particularly if global tensions ease.

items.headline: Fed Beige Book Released, Key Economic Data Ahead
items.summary: The Federal Reserve released its June Beige Book today, offering qualitative insights into economic conditions across its districts. Recent JOLTS data indicated an increase in US job openings to 7.618 million in April, suggesting a hawkish stance on the Fed's rate path. Additionally, the May ISM Services data and ADP employment report are expected today, while Eurozone inflation rose to 3.2% in May, potentially prompting an ECB rate hike on June 11.

items.headline: Macy's Beats Expectations, Broadcom Reports After Hours
items.summary: Macy's Inc. ($M) reported strong first-quarter 2026 results before market open, with comparable sales up 3.0% and a raised full-year outlook, leading to a 3% pre-market rise in shares. Broadcom Inc. ($AVGO), whose earnings are closely watched as a barometer for AI infrastructure investment, is scheduled to release its second-quarter earnings after today's market close. Medtronic ($MDT) is also among companies reporting earnings today.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Escalate, US Proposes New Tariffs
items.summary: Geopolitical instability continues with escalating tensions in the Middle East, contributing to higher crude oil prices. Concurrently, the US has proposed new tariffs of 10-12.5% on imports from 60 economies, including Japan, citing inadequate measures against goods produced with forced labor. The OECD has also lowered its 2026 global economic growth forecast to 2.8%.
